verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein produced by the epididymal epithelium with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or other membrane-anchoring motif of its own. It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and associates with the sperm surface post-translationally. Critically, the dominant (protein D) form maintains only a reversible, equilibrium-based interaction with the sperm surface and is removed by a single wash. The minor (protein E) form is more tightly bound, but this is a post-translational non-covalent association with an exogenous cell surface rather than an intrinsic anchoring mechanism of the CRISP1 protein body. There is no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no covalent linkage to a TM scaffold, and no evidence that CRISP1 on a somatic cell surface is relevant to therapeutic targeting. This is secreted_only biology: CRISP1 reaches the sperm surface via reversible non-covalent recruitment, not via its own stable membrane-anchoring mechanism. The wash test fails for the dominant form.
